human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cage-specific workflow depth, cash and account evidence quality, variance and completion control discipline, AML and credit boundary posture, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cage, vault, cashier, chip, voucher, kiosk, patron-funds, and wagering-account exception handling rather than credit approval, AML disposition, accounting write-offs, or legal interpretation.

Authority sources cover NIGC cage and vault controls, Nevada cage and credit MICS, Nevada CPA cage checklists, New Jersey casino and chip rules, FinCEN casino recordkeeping guidance, and GLI cashless controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal cage execution, missing evidence, unsupported backdating, completion without proof, accountability mismatches, suspicious cash escalation, adjacent-lane rejection, policy conflicts, and tenant-specific jurisdiction adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/casinos-gaming-and-wagering/cage-operations-specialist/ so cage work is distinct from adjacent credit, compliance, AML, surveillance, accounting, and sportsbook lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported corrections, false completion attempts, suspicious cash activity, credit or licensing overreach, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated cage operations specialist for cash, voucher, kiosk, patron-funds, and balancing execution with explicit cash, account, and compliance boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
